articles.title: As India, US head towards a new relationship, here are the numbers that matter
articles.description: India-US trade deal: The framework targets balanced trade, supply chain resilience and deeper technology cooperation. India intends to buy $500 billion of US energy & tech products, aircraft & parts, precious metals, tech products, coking coal over 5 years.
articles.content:

articles.title: Old Software You've Never Heard Of That Kept Businesses Running During the AWS Outage
articles.description: After nine years of pursuit, a CEO finally acquired InfoScale — and bet his company's future on a technology that stitches together the chaos of m...
articles.content: There's a particular kind of technology company that hides in plain sight. It doesn't chase viral moments or splash across keynote stages. Its logo doesn't grace consumer devices. Yet when the world's largest cloud provider goes dark — when hospitals... [17332 chars]

articles.title: Coastal States to lead India’s ‘Blue Economy’ journey: Dr Jitendra
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent VIJAYAWADA, Feb 7: Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences, and MoS PMO, Personnel, Public Grievances, Pensions, Atomic Energy and Space, Dr. Jitendra Singhtoday stated that Andhra Pradesh, along with other coastal states, will emerge as the torchbearer of India’s Blue Economy transformation. Referring to the Prime Minister’s repeated emphasis from the Red Fort on making the Blue Economy a national priority mission, the Minister said that the Union Budget 2026-27 gives concrete […]
articles.content:

articles.content: Bengaluru: As the city continues to attract global talent in technology/manufacturing sectors and startups, the challenge of integrating expatriates into India's business, regulatory and cultural ecosystem is becoming increasingly visible. Aiming to ... [2152 chars]

articles.title: NIT Srinagar student shines at All India Inter NIT Chess Meet
articles.description: Excelsior Sports Correspondent SRINAGAR, Feb 7: A student of the National Institute of Technology (NIT) Srinagar has secured second place at the All India Inter NIT Chess and Athletics Tournament held at NIT Jalandhar. Satinder Singh finished second on Board 1, competing against top players from NITs across the country. Director NIT Srinagar, Prof Binod Kumar Kanaujia, said the achievement reflects the institute’s emphasis on promoting sports alongside academics. Incharge Registrar Prof Atikur Rehman and Dean Students Welfare (DSW), Prof […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Sports Correspondent
SRINAGAR, Feb 7: A student of the National Institute of Technology (NIT) Srinagar has secured second place at the All India Inter NIT Chess and Athletics Tournament held at NIT Jalandhar.
Satinder Singh finished second ... [793 chars]

articles.title: Heritage School students gain exposure at IIT Jammu
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent JAMMU, Feb 7: Heritage School organised an educational visit to the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu as part of PRAGYAAN 2026, the institute’s flagship outreach and innovation event. A group of 55 students from Classes IX and XI, accompanied by science faculty members, participated in the visit aimed at familiarising them with higher education, research and technological innovation. A major attraction was the painting competition “The Open Palette”, where students showcased their creativity and imagination in an inspiring […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Correspondent
JAMMU, Feb 7: Heritage School organised an educational visit to the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu as part of PRAGYAAN 2026, the institute’s flagship outreach and innovation event. A group of 55 students from Classes IX ... [893 chars]

articles.title: Brussel's Brexit revenge: EU tells Britain it must agree to 'onerous terms' if it want to join £130billion defence fund
articles.description: British firms will be forced to source key technology and parts from Europe if Sir Keir Starmer signs up to a £130 billion EU defence fund, The Mail on Sunday can reveal.
articles.content:
British firms will be forced to source key technology and parts from Europe if Sir Keir Starmer signs up to a £130 billion EU defence fund, The Mail on Sunday can reveal.
The clause – slipped into the small print at a backroom meeting in Brussels las... [3283 chars]

articles.title: India eyes to buy US goods worth $500 bn over next 5 years
articles.description: Energy products, aircraft, technology part of a broader push to deepen economic, strategic and technology cooperation under the interim trade agreement framework with the US.
articles.content: India has signalled its intent to purchase $500 billion worth of US energy products, aircraft, technology goods and other commodities over the next five years as part of a broader push to deepen economic, strategic and technology cooperation under th... [2794 chars]

articles.title: IHBT team reviews progress on bioresource project in Mizoram
articles.description: A team of scientists from the CSIR-Institute of Himalayan Bioresource Technology (CSIR-IHBT), Palampur, visited Aizawl in Mizoram to review progress on research and extension activities under the project titled “Inter-Institutional Programme Support on the Development and Sustainable Bioresources of Mizoram,” funded by the Department of Biotechnology (DBT) of the Union Government.
articles.content: A team of scientists from the CSIR-Institute of Himalayan Bioresource Technology (CSIR-IHBT), Palampur, visited Aizawl in Mizoram to review progress on research and extension activities under the project titled “Inter-Institutional Programme Support ... [1797 chars]

articles.title: India To Have Trade Deficit With US From Next Year; To Remove Non-Tariff Barriers On Agri Imports
articles.description: The framework statement issued by the White House said that India intends to purchase $500 billion of U.S. energy products, aircraft and aircraft parts, precious metals, technology products, and coking coal over the next 5 years.
articles.content: Chennai: Going by the joint statement on the interim framework on trade deal released on Saturday, India’s trade surplus with the US will turn into a trade deficit as it will have to buy goods worth $100 billion every year for the next five years. Th... [2488 chars]
